heading: Discretion of student financial aid administrators
label: 1087tt Discretion of student financial aid administrators
notes: Amendment of Subsection (b)(1)(B), , , provided that, effective on , subsection (b)(1)(B) of this section is amended as follows:Pub. L. 119–21, title VIII, § 83001(a)(3)139 Stat. 350July 4, 2025July 1, 2026(1) by striking clause (v); and(2) by redesignating clauses (vi) and (vii) as clauses (v) and (vi), respectively.See 2025 Amendment note below.

Prior ProvisionsA prior section 1087tt, , as added , , , related to discretion of student financial aid administrators under this part, prior to repeal by .Pub. L. 89–329, title IV, § 479APub. L. 99–498, title IV, § 406(a)100 Stat. 1472section 14(26) of Pub. L. 100–50Oct. 17, 1986

Amendments2025—Subsec. (b)(1)(B)(v) to (vii). redesignated cls. (vi) and (vii) as (v) and (vi), respectively, and struck out former cl. (v) which read as follows: “receipt of foreign income of permanent residents or United States citizens exempt from Federal taxation, or the foreign income for which a permanent resident or citizen received a foreign tax credit;”.Pub. L. 119–212020— amended section generally. Prior to amendment, section related to discretion of student financial aid administrators to take special circumstances into account and to make adjustments to cost of attendance or values of data items required to calculate expected student and/or parent contribution.Pub. L. 116–2602014—Subsec. (a). substituted “a dislocated worker (as defined in )” for “a dislocated worker (as defined in )”.Pub. L. 113–128section 3102 of title 29section 2801 of title 292008—Subsec. (a). substituted “medical, dental, or nursing home expenses” for “medical or dental expenses”, inserted “or dependent care” after “child care” and “student or” before “family member who is a dislocated worker”, and substituted “In addition, nothing in this subchapter shall be interpreted as limiting the authority of the student financial aid administrator in such cases (1) to request and use supplementary information about the financial status or personal circumstances of eligible applicants in selecting recipients and determining the amount of awards under this subchapter, or (2) to offer a dependent student financial assistance under or a Federal Direct Unsubsidized Stafford Loan without requiring the parents of such student to file the financial aid form prescribed under if the student financial aid administrator verifies that the parent or parents of such student have ended financial support of such student and refuse to file such form.” for “In addition, nothing in this subchapter shall be interpreted as limiting the authority of the student financial aid administrator in such cases to request and use supplementary information about the financial status or personal circumstances of eligible applicants in selecting recipients and determining the amount of awards under this subchapter.”Pub. (a). , in third sentence, substituted “family member or an independent student, a family member who is a dislocated worker (as defined in ), the number of parents” for “family member, the number of parents” and inserted “a change in housing status that results in an individual being homeless (as defined in ),” after “under ,”.Pub. L. 110–84section 2801 of title 29section 11302 of title 42section 1094 of this title1998—Subsec. (a). , inserted after second sentence “Special circumstances may include tuition expenses at an elementary or secondary school, medical or dental expenses not covered by insurance, unusually high child care costs, recent unemployment of a family member, the number of parents enrolled at least half-time in a degree, certificate, or other program leading to a recognized educational credential at an institution with a program participation agreement under , or other changes in a family’s income, a family’s assets, or a student’s status.”Pub. L. 105–244, § 478(1)section 1094 of this titleSubsec. (c). , amended heading and text of subsec. (c) generally. Prior to amendment, subsec. (c) related to student financial aid administrators making adjustments for special circumstances.Pub. L. 105–244, § 478(2)1993—Subsec. (c). added subsec. (c).Pub. L. 103–2081992— amended section generally, revising and restating provisions of subsecs. (a) and (b) and striking out former subsec. (c) which related to asset adjustment as example.Pub. L. 102–3251989—Subsec. (a). amended subsec. (a) generally. Prior to amendment, subsec. (a) read as follows: “Nothing in this subchapter shall be interpreted as limiting the authority of the student financial aid administrator, on the basis of adequate documentation, to make necessary adjustments to the cost of attendance and expected student or parent contribution (or both) to allow for treatment of individual students with special circumstances. In addition, nothing in this subchapter shall be interpreted as limiting the authority of the student financial aid administrator to use supplementary information about the financial status or personal circumstance of eligible applicants in selecting recipients and determining the amount of awards under subparts 1 and 2 of part A and parts B, C, and E of this subchapter.”Pub. L. 101–239

children.label: i
children.text: the cost of attendance;

children.label: ii
children.text: the values of the data used to calculate the student aid index; or

children.label: iii
children.text: the values of the data used to calculate the Federal Pell Grant award.

children.label: B
children.text: For an applicant with unusual circumstances under subsection (c), to the dependency status of such applicant.

children.heading: Use of authority
children.label: A Use of authority
children.text: No institution of higher education or financial aid administrator shall maintain a policy of denying all requests for adjustments under this section.

children.heading: No additional fee
children.label: B No additional fee
children.text: No student or parent shall be charged a fee for a documented interview of the student by the financial aid administrator or for the review of a student or parent’s request for adjustments under this section including the review of any supplementary information or documentation of a student or parent’s special circumstances or a student’s unusual circumstances.

children.heading: Rule of construction
children.label: C Rule of construction
children.text: The authority to make adjustments under paragraph (1)(A) shall not be construed to permit financial aid administrators to deviate from the cost of attendance, the values of data used to calculate the student aid index or the values of data used to calculate the Federal Pell Grant award (or both) for awarding aid under this subchapter in the absence of special circumstances.

children.label: A
children.text: a documented interview between the student and the financial aid administrator;

children.label: i
children.text: submission of a court order or official Federal or State documentation that the student or the student’s parents or legal guardians are incarcerated in any Federal or State penal institution;

children.label: I
children.text: a child welfare agency authorized by a State or county;

children.label: II
children.text: a Tribal welfare authority or agency;

children.label: III
children.text: an independent living case worker, such as a case worker who supports current and former foster youth with the transition to adulthood; or

children.label: IV
children.text: a public or private agency, facility, or program servicing the victims of abuse, neglect, assault, or violence, which may include domestic violence;

children.label: iii
children.text: a documented phone call or a written statement from an attorney, a guardian ad litem, or a court-appointed special advocate, or a person serving in a similar capacity which confirms the specific unusual circumstances and documents the person’s relationship to the student;

children.label: iv
children.text: a documented phone call or written statement from a representative under division 1 or 2 of subpart 2 of part A, which confirms the specific unusual circumstances and documents the representative’s relationship to the student;

children.label: v
children.text: documents, such as utility bills or health insurance documentation, that demonstrate a separation from parents or legal guardians; and

children.label: vi
children.text: in the absence of documentation described in this subparagraph, other documentation the financial aid administrator determines is adequate to confirm the unusual circumstances, pursuant to ; andsection 1087vv(d)(9) of this title

children.label: C
children.text: supplementary information, as necessary, about the financial status or personal circumstances of eligible applicants as it relates to the special circumstances or unusual circumstances based on which the applicant is requesting an adjustment.

children.heading: Special rule
children.label: 4 Special rule
children.text: In making adjustments under paragraph (1), a financial aid administrator may offer a dependent student financial assistance under a Federal Direct Unsubsidized Stafford Loan without requiring the parents of such student to provide their parent information on the Free Application for Federal Student Aid if the student does not qualify for, or does not choose to use, the unusual circumstance option described in , and the financial aid administrator determines that the parents of such student ended financial support of such student or refuse to file such form.section 1087vv(d)(9) of this title

children.heading: Public disclosure
children.label: 5 Public disclosure
children.text: Each institution of higher education shall make publicly available information that students applying for aid under this subchapter have the opportunity to pursue adjustments under this section.

children.heading: Adjustments for students with special circumstances
children.label: b Adjustments for students with special circumstances

children.heading: Special circumstances for adjustments related to Pell Grants
children.label: 1 Special circumstances for adjustments related to Pell Grants
A
children.text: shall be conditions that differentiate an individual student from a group of students rather than conditions that exist across a group of students; and

children.label: i
children.text: recent unemployment of a family member or student;

children.label: ii
children.text: a student or family member who is a dislocated worker (as defined in );section 3102 of title 29

children.label: iii
children.text: a change in housing status that results in an individual being a homeless youth;

children.label: iv
children.text: an unusual amount of claimed losses against income on the Federal tax return that substantially lower adjusted gross income, such as business, investment, or real estate losses;

children.label: v
children.text: receipt of foreign income of permanent residents or United States citizens exempt from Federal taxation, or the foreign income for which a permanent resident or citizen received a foreign tax credit;

children.label: vi
children.text: in the case of an applicant who does not qualify for the exemption from asset reporting under , assets as defined in ; orsection 1087ss of this titlesection 1087vv(f) of this title

children.label: vii
children.text: other changes or adjustments in the income, assets, or size of a family, or a student’s dependency status.

children.heading: Special circumstances for adjustments related to cost of attendance and student aid index
children.label: 2 Special circumstances for adjustments related to cost of attendance and student aid index
A
children.text: shall be conditions that differentiate an individual student from a group of students rather than conditions that exist across a group of students, except as provided in sections 1087uu and 1087uu–1 of this title; and

children.label: i
children.text: tuition expenses at an elementary school or secondary school;

children.label: ii
children.text: medical, dental, or nursing home expenses not covered by insurance;

children.label: iii
children.text: child care or dependent care costs not covered by the dependent care cost allowance calculated in accordance with section 1087 of this title;ll

children.label: iv
children.text: recent unemployment of a family member or student;

children.label: v
children.text: a student or family member who is a dislocated worker (as defined in );section 3102 of title 29

children.label: vi
children.text: the existence of additional family members enrolled in a degree, certificate, or other program leading to a recognized educational credential at an institution with a program participation agreement under ;section 1094 of this title

children.label: vii
children.text: a change in housing status that results in an individual being a homeless youth;

children.label: viii
children.text: a condition of severe disability of the student, or in the case of a dependent student, the dependent student’s parent or guardian, or in the case of an independent student, the independent student’s dependent or spouse;

children.label: ix
children.text: unusual amount of claimed losses against income on the Federal tax return that substantially lower adjusted gross income, such as business, investment, or real estate losses; or

children.label: x
children.text: other changes or adjustments in the income, assets, or size of a family, or a student’s dependency status.

children.label: A
children.text: conditions that differentiate an individual student from a group of students; and

children.label: B
children.text: based on unusual circumstances, pursuant to .section 1087vv(d)(9) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: enable each student who, based on an unusual circumstance described in , may qualify for an adjustment under subsection (a)(1)(B) that will result in a determination of independence under this section or to complete the Free Application for Federal Student Aid as an independent student for the purpose of a provisional determination of the student’s Federal financial aid award, with the final determination of the award subject to the documentation requirements of subsection (a)(3);section 1087vv(d)(9) of this titlesection 1087uu–2 of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: upon completion of the Free Application for Federal Student Aid provide an estimate of the student’s Federal Pell Grant award, and other information as specified in , based on the assumption that the student is determined to be an independent student; andsection 1090(a)(3)(A) of this title

children.label: iii
children.text: specify, on the Free Application for Federal Student Aid, the consequences under of knowingly and willfully completing the Free Application for Federal Student Aid as an independent student under clause (i) without meeting the unusual circumstances to qualify for such a determination.section 1097(a) of this title

children.heading: Requirements for financial aid administrators
children.label: B Requirements for financial aid administrators
i
children.text: notify the student of the institutional process, requirements, and timeline for an adjustment under this section and that will result in a review of the student’s request for an adjustment and a determination of the student’s dependency status under such sections within a reasonable time after the student completes the Free Application for Federal Student Aid;section 1087vv(d)(9) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: provide the student a final determination of the student’s dependency status and Federal financial aid award as soon as practicable after all requested documentation is provided;

children.label: iii
children.text: retain all documents related to the adjustment under this section and , including documented interviews, for at least the duration of the student’s enrollment, and shall abide by all other record keeping requirements of this chapter; andsection 1087vv(d)(9) of this title

children.label: I
children.text: the student informs the institution that circumstances have changed; or

children.label: II
children.text: the institution has specific conflicting information about the student’s independence.

children.heading: Eligibility
children.label: C Eligibility
children.text: If a student pursues provisional independent student status and is not determined to be an independent student by a financial aid administrator, such student shall only be eligible for a Federal Direct Unsubsidized Stafford Loan for that award year unless such student subsequently completes the Free Application for Federal Student Aid as a dependent student.

children.heading: Adjustments to assets or income taken into account
children.label: d Adjustments to assets or income taken into account

children.text: the administrator makes adjustments excluding from family income or assets any proceeds or losses from a sale of farm or business assets of a family if such sale results from a voluntary or involuntary foreclosure, forfeiture, or bankruptcy or a voluntary or involuntary liquidation; or

the administrator makes adjustments for a condition of disability of a student, or in the case of a dependent student, the dependent student’s parent or guardian, or in the case of an independent student, the independent student’s dependent or spouse, so as to take into consideration the additional costs incurred as a result of such disability.

children.heading: Refusal or adjustment of loan certifications
children.label: e Refusal or adjustment of loan certifications
children.text: On a case-by-case basis, an eligible institution may refuse to use the authority provided under this section, certify a statement that permits a student to receive a loan under part D, certify a loan amount, or make a loan that is less than the student’s determination of need (as determined under this part), if the reason for the action is documented and provided in writing to the student. No eligible institution shall discriminate against any borrower or applicant in obtaining a loan on the basis of race, ethnicity, national origin, religion, sex, marital status, age, or disability status.

children.heading: Special rule regarding professional judgment during a disaster, emergency, or economic downturn
children.label: f Special rule regarding professional judgment during a disaster, emergency, or economic downturn
A
children.text: determine that the income earned from work for an applicant is zero, if the applicant can provide paper or electronic documentation of receipt of unemployment benefits or confirmation that an application for unemployment benefits was submitted; and

children.label: B
children.text: make additional appropriate adjustments to the income earned from work for a student, parent, or spouse, as applicable, based on the totality of the family’s situation, including consideration of unemployment benefits.

children.heading: Documentation
children.label: 2 Documentation
children.text: For the purposes of documenting unemployment under paragraph (1), documentation shall be accepted if such documentation is submitted not more than 90 days from the date on which such documentation was issued, except if a financial aid administrator knows that the student, parent, or spouse, as applicable, has already obtained other employment.

children.heading: Program reviews
children.label: 3 Program reviews
children.text: The Secretary shall make adjustments to the model used to select institutions of higher education participating under this subchapter for program reviews in order to account for any rise in the use of professional judgment under this section during the award years applicable to the qualifying emergency, as determined by the Secretary.

children.label: A
children.text: an event for which the President declared a major disaster or an emergency under section 5170 or 5191, respectively, of title 42;

children.label: B
children.text: a national emergency related to the coronavirus declared by the President under ; orsection 1621 of title 50

children.label: C
children.text: a period of recession or economic downturn as determined by the Secretary, in consultation with the Secretary of Labor.
